channel.title: Wait, WHAT?
channel.description: "Wait, What?" is a podcast for curious kids who want to discover how the world really works. In each episode, we dive into a fascinating topic, uncover surprising facts, tell funny stories, and open our minds to new ideas. The podcast is part of HAMAL 4 Kids — a community project bringing light, attention, and quality content to kids in Israel, all year round. 🎧 Hit play and join us on a journey of wonder, wisdom, and imagination. ❤ Want to help us keep going? Buy us coffee on hamal4kids.online Note: This podcast is generated using AI, so some pronunciation m
channel.author: Hamal4kids
channel.itunes:author: Hamal4kids
channel.itunes:summary: "Wait, What?" is a podcast for curious kids who want to discover how the world really works. In each episode, we dive into a fascinating topic, uncover surprising facts, tell funny stories, and open our minds to new ideas. The podcast is part of HAMAL 4 Kids — a community project bringing light, attention, and quality content to kids in Israel, all year round. 🎧 Hit play and join us on a journey of wonder, wisdom, and imagination. ❤ Want to help us keep going? Buy us coffee on hamal4kids.online Note: This podcast is generated using AI, so some pronunciation m

item.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Philosophical Guide to Life
item.description:
Why does Sherlock Holmes care about the dirt on someone’s shoes? How do philosophers think differently from other people? And what does all this have to do with the meaning of life?

In this episode, we follow the sharpest detective in history — and learn how careful observation and philosophical thinking go hand in hand!

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free community project offering curious Hebrew content for children.

Listen to the full episode here
☕ Buy us a coffee

💡 This podcast was generated with AI and may contain pronunciation errors — we recommend co-listening with your kids.
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids
item.itunes:summary:
Why does Sherlock Holmes care about the dirt on someone’s shoes? How do philosophers think differently from other people? And what does all this have to do with the meaning of life?

In this episode, we follow the sharpest detective in history — and learn how careful observation and philosophical thinking go hand in hand!

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free community project offering curious Hebrew content for children.

Listen to the full episode here
☕ Buy us a coffee

💡 This podcast was generated with AI and may contain pronunciation errors — we recommend co-listening with your kids.

item.title: A Journey into Mythologies: Through the Worlds of Ancient Imagination
item.description:
Who invented Medusa? Why do dragons have wings? And how come ancient myths from around the world tell such similar stories?

In this first episode of Wait, What?! we travel across time and imagination — to meet gods, monsters, and some very clever kids.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free community project offering curious Hebrew content for children.

Listen to the full episode here
☕ Buy us a coffee

💡 This podcast was generated with AI and may contain pronunciation errors — we recommend co-listening with your kids.
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids
item.itunes:summary:
Who invented Medusa? Why do dragons have wings? And how come ancient myths from around the world tell such similar stories?

In this first episode of Wait, What?! we travel across time and imagination — to meet gods, monsters, and some very clever kids.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free community project offering curious Hebrew content for children.

Listen to the full episode here
☕ Buy us a coffee

💡 This podcast was generated with AI and may contain pronunciation errors — we recommend co-listening with your kids.

item.title: The Long, Weird History of Chocolate
item.description:
Once upon a time, chocolate wasn’t candy.
It wasn’t sweet, brown, or in little squares.
So what was it? In this episode, we discover the surprising origins of chocolate — and how it became the treat we love today.

This podcast is part of “Hamal for Kids” — a free community project.


Buy us a coffee ☕
This podcast is AI-generated and may include pronunciation errors — co-listening with children is
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids
item.itunes:summary:
Once upon a time, chocolate wasn’t candy.
It wasn’t sweet, brown, or in little squares.

This podcast is part of “Hamal for Kids” — a free community project.


Buy us a coffee ☕
This podcast is AI-generated and may include pronunciation errors — co-listening with children is

item.title: Mosasaurs: Rulers of the Ancient Seas
item.description:
They were massive, dangerous, and ruled the oceans while dinosaurs ruled the land.
But what exactly were mosasaurs? Fish? Lizards? Crocodiles?
And what kind of creature can swallow a shark for breakfast?

Dive into a new Wait, What? episode and discover the truth behind these sea monsters.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more (in Hebrew)
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This episode was created using AI and may contain pronunciation errors. We recommend co-listening with your child.
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids
item.itunes:summary:
They were massive, dangerous, and ruled the oceans while dinosaurs ruled the land.
But what exactly were mosasaurs? Fish? Lizards? Crocodiles?
And what kind of creature can swallow a shark for breakfast?

Dive into a new Wait, What? episode and discover the truth behind these sea monsters.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more (in Hebrew)
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This episode was created using AI and may contain pronunciation errors. We recommend co-listening with your child.

item.title: Pterodactyl: Not a Dinosaur, But Still Awesome
item.description:
They looked like dragons, soared above the dinosaurs, and had enormous wings —
but were they really dinosaurs?
And could they have once flown right over where we live?

In this Wait, What? episode, we’ll learn what a Pterodactyl actually was, how it flew, and what it did with a toothless beak.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This episode was created using AI and may contain pronunciation errors. We recommend co-listening with your child.
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids
item.itunes:summary:
They looked like dragons, soared above the dinosaurs, and had enormous wings —
but were they really dinosaurs?
And could they have once flown right over where we live?

In this Wait, What? episode, we’ll learn what a Pterodactyl actually was, how it flew, and what it did with a toothless beak.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This episode was created using AI and may contain pronunciation errors. We recommend co-listening with your child.

item.title: T. rex: A Predator You Shouldn’t Mess With
item.description:
T. rex was one of the scariest dinosaurs ever — huge, strong, loud, and a skilled hunter.
But what made it such a top predator?
Did it hunt alone? What did it eat? And how do scientists even know?

Listen to this new Wait, What? episode and uncover the real story behind the teeth.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more
Want to support us?https://payboxapp.page.link/KzAVcfYv9A3RQs8w5 ☕
Note: This episode was created using AI and may contain pronunciation errors. We recommend co-listening with your child.
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids

item.title: Velociraptor: Smart, Small, and Dangerous
item.description:
Velociraptor: Smart, Small, and Dangerous
Fast, clever, sharp — and about the size of a chicken.
So why do we remember it as a movie monster?
And what really made it so dangerous?

Come meet the dino you really wouldn’t want to meet alone in the forest…

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This episode was created using AI and may contain pronunciation errors. We recommend co-listening with your child.
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids
item.itunes:summary:
Velociraptor: Smart, Small, and Dangerous
Fast, clever, sharp — and about the size of a chicken.
So why do we remember it as a movie monster?

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This episode was created using AI and may contain pronunciation errors. We recommend co-listening with your child.

item.title: Triceratops: A Rhino with a Frill
item.description:
Three horns, a giant frill, and the body of a prehistoric rhino — the Triceratops looked scary, but it was a plant-eater!
So how did it survive near T. rexes? And why did it need all those horns?

Press play for a surprising new episode in the Wait, What? dinosaur series.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This podcast was generated with AI, so some pronunciations might be off. We recommend co-listening with your child.
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids
item.itunes:summary:
Three horns, a giant frill, and the body of a prehistoric rhino — the Triceratops looked scary, but it was a plant-eater!
So how did it survive near T. rexes? And why did it need all those horns?

Press play for a surprising new episode in the Wait, What? dinosaur series.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This podcast was generated with AI, so some pronunciations might be off. We recommend co-listening with your child.

item.title: Spinosaurus: A Fish? A Crocodile? A Dinosaur!
item.description:
It hunted fish, loved the water, sometimes walked on all fours — and was one of the weirdest dinos ever.
With a sail on its back, a tail for swimming, and extra-sharp teeth... what was it really?

Was it a fish? A crocodile? Or just a very strange dinosaur? Listen and find out!

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This podcast was generated with AI, so some pronunciations might be off. We recommend co-listening with your child.
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids
item.itunes:summary:
It hunted fish, loved the water, sometimes walked on all fours — and was one of the weirdest dinos ever.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This podcast was generated with AI, so some pronunciations might be off. We recommend co-listening with your child.

item.title: Stegosaurus: Walking Wall of Armor
item.description:
With big plates on its back and spikes on its tail, the Stegosaurus looked like a mix between a dinosaur and a fence!
But why did it have all those decorations? And how did it defend itself?

Listen to a new episode of Wait, What? and discover the secrets of one of the weirdest dinos in history.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode and more (Hebrew)
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This podcast was generated with AI, so some pronunciations might be off. We recommend co-listening with your child.
item.dc:creator: Hamal4kids

With big plates on its back and spikes on its tail, the Stegosaurus looked like a mix between a dinosaur and a fence!

Full episode and more (Hebrew)
Want to support us? Buy us a coffee ☕
Note: This podcast was generated with AI, so some pronunciations might be off. We recommend co-listening with your child.

item.title: Ankylosaurus: A Dinosaur Tank
item.description:
Ankylosaurus: Armor on Legs
Who tried to attack it? Why did it have a tail like a hammer? And how did it survive in a world full of T. rexes?

In this episode of Wait, What? we meet one of the best-protected dinosaurs of all time.
Press play and discover what it was like to be a walking tank in the age of dinosaurs!

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.
Full episode (in Hebrew) and surprises
Want to support us?https://payboxapp.page.link/KzAVcfYv9A3RQs8w5 ☕
Note: This podcast was generated with AI, so some pronunciations might be off. We recommend co-listening with your child.

Who tried to attack it? Why did it have a tail like a hammer? And how did it survive in a world full of T. rexes?

In this episode of Wait, What? we meet one of the best-protected dinosaurs of all time.
Press play and discover what it was like to be a walking tank in the age of dinosaurs!

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered content project for curious Israeli children.

Note: This podcast was generated with AI, so some pronunciations might be off. We recommend co-listening with your child.

item.title: Brachiosaurus: What’s Up With That Neck?
item.description:
How do you reach the highest leaves? Just ask the Brachiosaurus — a giant dinosaur with a loooong neck that stretched toward the sky.
Find out why it was so tall, what it ate, and how it managed to live that way.

This podcast is part of Hamal for Kids — a free, community-powered project bringing curious and fun content to children in Israel.
Full episode and more surprises
Want to support us? https://payboxapp.page.link/KzAVcfYv9A3RQs8w5 ☕
Note: This episode was created using AI tools and may contain pronunciation errors. We recommend co-listening and helping your child with correct pronunciation.

item.title: The Diplodocus: The Longest Dinosaur That Ever Lived
item.description:
Meet the Diplodocus — the longest dinosaur ever!
How did it really look? Why did it have such a long neck? And what’s the deal with that giant tail?
Press play and travel back in time with us!

Full episode post (in Hebrew)

This episode is part of the dinosaur series from “Wait, what?”, a podcast by Hamal for Kids — a free community project bringing curious and funny content to Israeli children.
Want to support us? ☕ Buy us a coffee!
Note: This podcast was created using AI, so some pronunciations may not be perfect. We recommend co-listening and guiding kids toward correct pronunciation.

How did it really look? Why did it have such a long neck? And what’s the deal with that giant tail?

This episode is part of the dinosaur series from “Wait, what?”, a podcast by Hamal for Kids — a free community project bringing curious and funny content to Israeli children.
Want to support us? ☕ Buy us a coffee!
Note: This podcast was created using AI, so some pronunciations may not be perfect. We recommend co-listening and guiding kids toward correct pronunciation.

item.title: Meet the Dinosaures
item.description:
Welcome to the first episode in our dinosaur series on “Wait, What?”
What did dinosaurs really look like? Why did some have feathers? And how do we even know about them?
This is just the beginning of an amazing prehistoric adventure!

This podcast is part of HAMAL 4 Kids — a community project that brings curious, funny, and free content to kids in Israel.
The full post with more cool facts and surprises is waiting for you at hamal4kids.online.

🎧 Press play and join the adventure!
☕ Want to help us? Buy us a coffee: hamal4kids.online

Please note: this podcast was made with AI, so there may be small mistakes in pronunciation.
We recommend listening together and helping your child with correct pronunciation if needed.
